steps.title: Identify the elaborating relationship
steps.content: The key signal: “continents” is immediately followed by “geological structures called arcs” — a more specific description that elaborates on what continents are in this geological context.

steps.title: Use a colon to mark elaboration
steps.content:
A colon introduces a phrase that elaborates on or clarifies the preceding noun. “Geological structures called arcs” elaborates on “continents,” requiring a colon.

No punctuation (A) fails to mark the relationship; a semicolon (C) and period (D) require independent clauses or sentence boundaries that aren't present.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
